Wordpress content 01

A Practical Guide to Sustainable Web Design

Every page we ship has a footprint. In this guide we look at how thoughtful design choices reduce that cost while making interfaces faster and easier to use. For the full background, read the original research — most of the ideas here build directly on it, and the core principle is surprisingly simple: send less, render sooner.

Sustainable design is not about doing less — it is about doing exactly what the user needs, and nothing more.

Why it matters

The web is the largest connected machine ever built, and most of its energy goes into moving and rendering bytes. Trimming the obvious waste — oversized images, unused scripts, redundant fonts — usually delivers the biggest wins before you ever touch the harder trade-offs.

  • Measure first: you cannot improve what you have not profiled.
  • Ship critical content as plain, semantic HTML.
  • Defer everything that is not needed for the first interaction.
    • Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
    • Split bundles so each route loads only its own code.
      • Prefer native elements over heavy widgets.

The fastest request is the one you never make. Before optimising a resource, ask whether it needs to exist at all.

Performance folklore, repeated often because it is true.

Core principles, step by step

A repeatable process beats heroics. The four steps below scale from a landing page to an entire platform.

  1. Audit the current page weight and set a budget.
  2. Cut the heaviest offenders, then re-measure.
    1. Compress and resize images to their display size.
    2. Subset fonts and load only the weights you use.
      1. Drop icon fonts in favour of inline SVG.
  3. Automate the budget in CI so regressions never ship.

Comparing the options

The table below summarises three common image strategies and how they trade off quality, weight and effort.

You can tap any photograph in this article to open it at full size — a small enhancement that keeps the layout calm while still giving curious readers the detail they want.

Putting it into practice

Side-by-side layouts help readers scan trade-offs quickly without scrolling back and forth.

Before

Three render-blocking scripts, an uncompressed hero image and four font weights loaded on every page.

After

One deferred script, an AVIF hero and two subsetted weights — roughly a third of the original weight.

Design and engineering, together

The best results come when designers set the budget and engineers protect it. Pair the two early and revisit the numbers at every review.

Text flows naturally around a left-aligned image, which is ideal for portraits or supporting visuals that should not interrupt the reading rhythm. Notice how the paragraph keeps its measure while the figure sits to the side. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Tincidunt lobortis feugiat vivamus at.

A right-aligned image mirrors the effect on the opposite side. Use the two sparingly and in alternation so the page keeps a steady left edge for the body copy. Quam vulputate dignissim suspendisse in est ante in nibh mauris. Interdum consectetur libero id faucibus nisl tincidunt eget. Ultrices vitae auctor eu augue.

Key facts read best as a compact two-column reference rather than buried in prose:

Watch it in action

Embedded media is convenient, but it is also heavy — load it on demand and always provide a caption for context.

When you control the source file, a native video avoids the third-party player entirely. It is the lightest option for short, muted loops and product demos.

Where to go next

Start small: pick one page, set a budget, and ship a measurable improvement this week. Momentum compounds.

Read the docs
See the checklist
Browse examples
Join the newsletter
A note on tooling

The techniques here are framework-agnostic. Whether you build with WordPress, a static site generator or a custom stack, the budget and the measurements are what keep you honest.

Get started

Figures in this article are illustrative. Methodology and sources are available on request, and all benchmarks were taken on a mid-range device over a throttled 4G connection to reflect real-world conditions rather than ideal ones.
